input.question:
We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Please list all rules relevant to `Powertrain`. Answer with only the rule numbers (i.e.: AA.1.1.1) separated by commas and no other words.



The rules relevant to `Powertrain` are:

retrieved_context:
RULE: d.2.3
            TITLE: powertrain operation

            CONTENT:
            rule D.2.3: Powertrain Operation
D.2.3 Powertrain Operation 
In the paddock, (IC) Engines may be run or (EV) Tractive System may be Active if all three:   
a. The vehicle has passed Technical Inspection up to and including the Tilt Test OR a 
Technical Inspector gives permission  
b. The vehicle is supported on a stand  
c. The drive wheels are minimum 10 cm off the ground, OR the drive wheels are removed
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 126 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: t.1.4.1
            TITLE: accelerator pedal

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.4.1: Accelerator Pedal
T.1.4.1 Accelerator Pedal 
a. An Accelerator Pedal must control the Powertrain output   
b. Pedal Travel is the percent of travel from a fully released position to a fully applied 
position.  0% is fully released and 100% is fully applied. 
c. The Accelerator Pedal must: 
• Return to 0% Pedal Travel when not pushed  
• Have a positive stop to prevent any cable, actuation system or sensor from damage 
or overstress

RULE: ev.3.3.3
            TITLE: the powertrain must not regenerate energy when vehicle speed is between 0 and 5 km/hr

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.3.3.3: The powertrain must not regenerate energy when vehicle speed is between 0 and 5 km/hr
EV.3.3.3 The powertrain must not regenerate energy when vehicle speed is between 0 and 5 km/hr

RULE: gr.6.4.2
            TITLE: rules specific to vehicles based on their powertrain will be specified as such in the rule text:

            CONTENT:
            rule GR.6.4.2: Rules specific to vehicles based on their powertrain will be specified as such in the rule text:
GR.6.4.2 Rules specific to vehicles based on their powertrain will be specified as such in the rule text: 
• Internal Combustion  “IC” or “IC Only” 
• Electric Vehicle  “EV” or “EV Only”

RULE: ic.1.1.2
            TITLE: hybrid powertrains, such as those using electric motors running off stored energy, are

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.1.1.2: Hybrid powertrains, such as those using electric motors running off stored energy, are
IC.1.1.2 Hybrid powertrains, such as those using electric motors running off stored energy, are 
prohibited.

RULE: t.5.6.1
            TITLE: the vehicle must have separate containers (catch cans) to retain fluids from any vents from

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.6.1: The vehicle must have separate containers (catch cans) to retain fluids from any vents from
T.5.6.1 The vehicle must have separate containers (catch cans) to retain fluids from any vents from 
the powertrain systems.

RULE: ev.3.2.3
            TITLE: all tractive system power must flow through the energy meter

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.3.2.3: All Tractive System power must flow through the Energy Meter
EV.3.2.3 All Tractive System power must flow through the Energy Meter

RULE: ev.7.7.4
            TITLE: the team must have a test to demonstrate bspd operation at electrical technical inspection.

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.7.4: The team must have a test to demonstrate BSPD operation at Electrical Technical Inspection.
EV.7.7.4 The team must have a test to demonstrate BSPD operation at Electrical Technical Inspection.   
a. Power must not be sent to the Motor(s) of the vehicle during the test  
b. The test must prove the function of the complete BSPD in the vehicle, including the 
current sensor 
The suggested test would introduce a current by a separate wire from an external power 
supply simulating the Tractive System current while pressing the brake pedal

RULE: t.5.2.1
            TITLE: exposed high speed final drivetrain equipment such as continuously variable transmissions

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.2.1: Exposed high speed final drivetrain equipment such as Continuously Variable Transmissions
T.5.2.1 Exposed high speed final drivetrain equipment such as Continuously Variable Transmissions 
(CVTs), sprockets, gears, pulleys, torque converters, clutches, belt drives, clutch drives and 
electric motors, must be fitted with scatter shields intended to contain drivetrain parts in case 
of radial failure

RULE: ic.1.1.1
            TITLE: the engine(s) used to power the vehicle must:

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.1.1.1: The engine(s) used to power the vehicle must:
IC.1.1.1 The engine(s) used to power the vehicle must: 
a. Be a piston engine(s) using a four stroke primary heat cycle  
b. Have a total combined displacement less than or equal to 710 cc per cycle.

RULE: ic.4.1.1
            TITLE: the electronic throttle must automatically close (return to idle) when power is removed.

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.4.1.1: The electronic throttle must automatically close (return to idle) when power is removed.
IC.4.1.1 The electronic throttle must automatically close (return to idle) when power is removed.

RULE: ic.4.1.2
            TITLE: the electronic throttle must use minimum two sources of energy capable of returning the

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.4.1.2: The electronic throttle must use minimum two sources of energy capable of returning the
IC.4.1.2 The electronic throttle must use minimum two sources of energy capable of returning the 
throttle to the idle position.  
a. One of the sources may be the device (such as a DC motor) that normally actuates the 
throttle 
b. The other device(s) must be a throttle return spring that can return the throttle to the 
idle position if loss of actuator power occurs.  
c. Springs in the TPS are not acceptable throttle return springs

RULE: d.13.2.4
            TITLE: vehicles must power down after leaving the course and be pushed to the fueling station or

            CONTENT:
            rule D.13.2.4: Vehicles must power down after leaving the course and be pushed to the fueling station or
D.13.2.4 Vehicles must power down after leaving the course and be pushed to the fueling station or 
data download area

RULE: ic.9.3.2
            TITLE: function - the primary master switch must:

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.9.3.2: Function - the Primary Master Switch must:
IC.9.3.2 Function - the Primary Master Switch must: 
a. Disconnect power to ALL electrical circuits, including the battery, alternator, lights, fuel 
pump(s), ignition and electrical controls.   
All battery current must flow through this switch 
b. Be direct acting, not act through a relay or logic.

RULE: t.1.4.4
            TITLE: all vehicle controls must stay below the top-most point of the front hoop in any operational

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.4.4: All Vehicle Controls must stay below the top-most point of the Front Hoop in any operational
T.1.4.4 All Vehicle Controls must stay below the top-most point of the Front Hoop in any operational 
position

RULE: t.2.1
            TITLE: harness definitions

            CONTENT:
            rule T.2.1: Harness Definitions
T.2.1 Harness Definitions 
a. 5 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts and one Anti-Submarine 
Belt.  
b. 6 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts and two leg or Anti-
Submarine Belts.  
c. 7 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts, two leg or Anti-
Submarine Belts and a negative g or Z Belt.   
d. Upright Driving Position - with a seat back angled at 30° or less from the vertical as 
measured along the line joining the two 200 mm circles of the template of the 95th 
percentile male as defined in F.5.6.4 and positioned per F.5.6.5 
e. Reclined Driving Position - with a seat back angled at more than 30° from the vertical as 
measured along the line joining the two 200 mm circles of the template of the 95th 
percentile male as defined in F.5.6.4 and positioned per F.5.6.5 
f. Chest to Groin Line - the straight line that in side view follows the line of the Shoulder 
Belts from the chest to the release buckle.

RULE: t.4.2.5
            TITLE: if an implausibility occurs between the values of the appss and persists for more than 100

            CONTENT:
            rule T.4.2.5: If an Implausibility occurs between the values of the APPSs and persists for more than 100
T.4.2.5 If an Implausibility occurs between the values of the APPSs and persists for more than 100 
msec, the power to the (IC) Electronic Throttle / (EV) Motor(s) must be immediately stopped 
completely.
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 65 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
(EV only) It is not necessary to Open the Shutdown Circuit, the motor controller(s) stopping 
the power to the Motor(s) is sufficient.

RULE: f.11.1.3
            TITLE: tractive system (ev.1.1) components including cables and wiring other than those in f.11.1.2

            CONTENT:
            rule F.11.1.3: Tractive System (EV.1.1) components including cables and wiring other than those in F.11.1.2
F.11.1.3 Tractive System (EV.1.1) components including cables and wiring other than those in F.11.1.2 
above must be contained inside one or both of: 
• The Rollover Protection Envelope F.1.13 
• Structure meeting F.5.16 Component Protection

RULE: f.11.2.2
            TITLE: rear impact protection

            CONTENT:
            rule F.11.2.2: Rear Impact Protection
F.11.2.2 Rear Impact Protection 
a. All Accumulator Containers must be protected from rear impact:  
• When the rear impact structure is 100 mm or less from an Accumulator Container, 
the structure must be Equivalent to Side Impact Structure (F.6.4, F.7.5)  
• When the rear impact structure is more than 100 mm from an Accumulator 
Container, the structure must meet  F.5.16 Component Protection 
• The Accumulator Container must not be part of the Equivalent structure. 
b. The Rear Impact Protection for the Accumulator Container must: 
• Go to the Upper Side Impact Height at minimum
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 53 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
• Have a structural and triangulated load path from the top of the Rear Impact 
Protection to the Upper Side Impact Structure  F.6.4, F.7.5 at the Main Hoop  
• Have a structural and triangulated load path from the bottom of the Rear Impact 
Protection to the Lower Side Impact Structure  F.6.4, F.7.5 at the Main Hoop  
c. Other Tractive System components in a position below 350 mm from the ground must be 
protected from rear impact by structure meeting F.5.16 Component Protection

RULE: ic.4.7.2
            TITLE: throttle position vs target

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.4.7.2: Throttle Position vs Target
IC.4.7.2 Throttle Position vs Target 
a. The power to the electronic throttle must be immediately shut down, if throttle position 
differs by more than 10% from the expected target TPS position for more than one 
second.  
b. An interval of one second is allowed for the difference to reduce to less than 10%, failure 
to achieve this in the required interval must result in immediate shut down of fuel flow 
and the ignition system. 
c. An error in TPS position and the resultant system shutdown must be demonstrated at 
Technical Inspection.   
Teams must have a method to demonstrate that the actions in IC.4.7.2.b above are met. 
System states displayed using calibration software must be accompanied by a detailed 
explanation of the control system.
